creator: horia@racoviceanu.com
text:
> Please see http://people.freebsd.org/~rakuco/fira-f-char.png for how
> "fi" and "fl" render with the Fira shipped in x11-fonts (Konsole,
> Qt-based, in the background, and Leafpad, GTK-based, in the foreground).
> It doesn't happen in all applications, though -- Emacs and lxterminal
> render those sequences just fine.

What I see is called standard typographic ligature:

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Typographic_ligature

"The feature replaces a sequence of glyphs with a single glyph
which is preferred for typographic purposes. This feature is
applied by default."

Any of the advanced Open Type typographic layout features can be
enabled or disabled by user or application using a tag system.

Additional Technical Documentation is not included in the port.
It's under a different license and I can't include it myself.

Copyright © 2013 by Carrois Type Design
No part of this publication may be reproduced, stored in a retrieval
system, or transmittet, in any form or by any means, electronic,
mecanical, photocopying, recording or otherwise, without the prior
written consent of the publisher

I have contacted both Mozilla and Ralph du Carrois about adding this
to MASTER_SITES. It's a matter of finding someone with access to their
Github account, which may take a while.

Thus I'm listing important features in pkg-message, with links for reference.
